title: CLAUDE.md — qte Package

title: CLAUDE.md — qte Package
text: two-period estimators share pipeline: QTEparams (QTEparams.R) — collects user arguments named list setupData (setupData.R) — extracts treated/untreated subsets ECDFs injects caller’s environment via assign(…, envir=env) env <- parent.frame(). side-effectful pattern intentional fragile; handle carefully. compute.* functions — statistical workhorse method; called point estimate inside bootstrap iteration bootstrap / bootiter (bootstrap.R) — empirical bootstrap SE QTE (qte.R) — S3 class constructor holding estimates CIs
code: User call (e.g., CiC) → QTEparams → setupData → compute.* → bootstrap → QTE

title: DiD-Based Quantile Treatment Effects
text: qte package provides six -based estimators QTT. share yname/gname/tname/idname interface support staggered treatment adoption natively. differ identification assumptions restrictions assumptions impose data-generating process. vignette describes estimator conceptually shows minimal call. full applied workflow aggregations event-study plots, see vignette("staggered-adoption"). use mpdta throughout: balanced panel 500 US counties observed annually 2003–2007, lemp (log employment) outcome first.treat cohort variable (year first minimum wage increase; 0 = never treated). common call structure six estimators :
code: library(qte) set.seed(42) data(mpdta, package = "did") estimator(   yname  = "lemp",        # outcome   gname  = "first.treat", # first treatment period (0 = never treated)   tname  = "year",        # calendar time   idname = "countyreal",  # unit id   data   = mpdta,   gt_type = "att",        # "att" or "qtt"   biters  = 100 )

title: DiD-Based Quantile Treatment Effects
text: Reference: Athey Imbens (2006) Identification assumption: rank unit untreated outcome distribution stable time (rank invariance), support treated group’s pre-period distribution contained support untreated group’s pre-period distribution (support condition). functional form outcome equation assumed. use: flexible nonparametric alternative linear parallel trends assumption implausible levels rank stability credible. Works panel repeated cross sections. Key restriction: Stronger standard parallel trends — requires entire rank ordering preserved, just mean.
code: res_cic <- cic(   yname  = "lemp", gname = "first.treat", tname = "year",   idname = "countyreal", data = mpdta,   gt_type = "att", biters = 50 ) summary(res_cic) #>  #> Overall ATT:   #>      ATT    Std. Error     [ 95%  Conf. Int.]  #>  -0.0197        0.0185    -0.0532      0.0139  #>  #>  #> Dynamic Effects: #>  Event Time Estimate Std. Error [95% Simult.  Conf. Band]   #>          -3   0.0508     0.0176        0.0164      0.0853 * #>          -2   0.0158     0.0159       -0.0154      0.0471   #>          -1  -0.0128     0.0156       -0.0434      0.0178   #>           0  -0.0081     0.0185       -0.0442      0.0281   #>           1  -0.0364     0.0196       -0.0748      0.0020   #>           2  -0.1226     0.0348       -0.1907     -0.0544 * #>           3  -0.0930     0.0369       -0.1653     -0.0206 * #> --- #> Signif. codes: `*' confidence band does not cover 0

title: DiD-Based Quantile Treatment Effects
text: Reference: Callaway et al. (2018) Identification assumption: Distributional parallel trends — entire untreated potential outcome distribution shifts parallel across time treated untreated groups (just mean). Formally, FY(0)|D=1,tF_{Y(0)|D=1,t} shifts time way FY(0)|D=0,tF_{Y(0)|D=0,t}. use: direct distributional analog standard . Weaker CiC require rank invariance, stronger mean . Works panel repeated cross sections. Key restriction: Requires full distributional shift parallel, just mean shift; testable pre-treatment periods.
code: res_qdid <- qdid(   yname  = "lemp", gname = "first.treat", tname = "year",   idname = "countyreal", data = mpdta,   gt_type = "att", biters = 50 ) summary(res_qdid) #>  #> Overall ATT:   #>      ATT    Std. Error     [ 95%  Conf. Int.]  #>  -0.0271        0.0136    -0.0567      0.0026  #>  #>  #> Dynamic Effects: #>  Event Time Estimate Std. Error [95% Simult.  Conf. Band]   #>          -3   0.0266     0.0145       -0.0018      0.0550   #>          -2  -0.0027     0.0143       -0.0308      0.0254   #>          -1  -0.0257     0.0143       -0.0538      0.0023   #>           0  -0.0148     0.0129       -0.0401      0.0105   #>           1  -0.0588     0.0196       -0.0972     -0.0203 * #>           2  -0.1366     0.0374       -0.2099     -0.0632 * #>           3  -0.1111     0.0400       -0.1895     -0.0328 * #> --- #> Signif. codes: `*' confidence band does not cover 0

title: DiD-Based Quantile Treatment Effects
text: Reference: Callaway et al. (2018) Identification assumption: Conditional distributional parallel trends — untreated potential outcome distribution shifts parallel conditional pre-treatment covariates. Extends QDiD allow covariate adjustment. use: distributional parallel trends holds conditioning covariates. covariate adjustment done via outcome regression CDFs, making flexible linear regression adjustment standard . Key restriction: spirit QDiD allows covariates. Requires panel data.

title: DiD-Based Quantile Treatment Effects
text: Reference: Callaway Li (2019) Identification assumption: Standard (mean) parallel trends — mean untreated potential outcome evolves parallel treated untreated groups. assumption standard . use: mean parallel trends credible, distributional parallel trends. mdid() recovers ATT weaker assumption can also return QTT combining mean rank-based counterfactual distribution. Key restriction: Weakest distributional assumption four methods. QTT estimate relies additional rank-stability within mean framework.
code: res_mdid <- mdid(   yname  = "lemp", gname = "first.treat", tname = "year",   idname = "countyreal", data = mpdta,   gt_type = "att", biters = 50 ) summary(res_mdid) #>  #> Overall ATT:   #>      ATT    Std. Error     [ 95%  Conf. Int.]   #>  -0.0305        0.0109    -0.0565     -0.0044 * #>  #>  #> Dynamic Effects: #>  Event Time Estimate Std. Error [95% Simult.  Conf. Band]   #>          -3   0.0298     0.0148        0.0007      0.0588 * #>          -2  -0.0024     0.0116       -0.0252      0.0203   #>          -1  -0.0243     0.0150       -0.0537      0.0051   #>           0  -0.0189     0.0100       -0.0386      0.0007   #>           1  -0.0536     0.0159       -0.0847     -0.0225 * #>           2  -0.1363     0.0373       -0.2093     -0.0632 * #>           3  -0.1008     0.0315       -0.1625     -0.0391 * #> --- #> Signif. codes: `*' confidence band does not cover 0

title: DiD-Based Quantile Treatment Effects
text: Reference: Callaway Li (2019) Identification assumption: Copula stability — dependence structure (copula) pre-treatment post-treatment untreated potential outcomes treated untreated groups. panel-data assumption uses three periods: two pre-treatment periods estimate copula, one post-treatment period. use: panel data least two pre-treatment periods per cohort available copula stability assumption credible. Uniquely identifies individual-level rank mapping pre post, enabling inference QTT weaker distributional assumptions CiC. Key restriction: Requires panel data sufficient pre-treatment periods. pre_copula argument controls whether fixed ("short") rolling ("long", default) base period used.

title: DiD-Based Quantile Treatment Effects
text: Identification assumption: Unconfoundedness conditional lagged outcome — treatment good randomly assigned conditioning pre-treatment value outcome. selection--observables assumption, parallel trends assumption. use: pre-treatment outcome strong proxy unobserved confounders driving selection treatment, parallel trends assumption implausible. Common labor health economics lagged earnings health status strong predictors treatment. Key restriction: require parallel trends, assumes conditioning lagged outcome absorbs confounding. Requires panel data.

title: Quantile Treatment Effects in R
text: Average Treatment Effect (ATE) Average Treatment Effect Treated (ATT) summarizes causal impact policy single number: mean difference outcomes treated untreated units. often natural quantity, can miss important heterogeneity. job training program substantially raises earnings bottom distribution little effect top, ATE obscures . minimum wage increase compresses wage distribution, average effect conceals compression. Quantile Treatment Effect level τ\tau QTE(τ)=QY(1)(τ)−QY(0)(τ)QTE(\tau) = Q_Y(1)(\tau) - Q_Y(0)(\tau), QY(d)(τ)Q_Y(d)(\tau) τ\tau-th quantile potential outcome distribution treatment status dd. Mapping QTE(τ)QTE(\tau) across τ∈(0,1)\tau \(0,1) traces treatment shifts entire outcome distribution. Quantile Treatment Effect Treated (QTTQTT) conditions treated group. vignette demonstrates unc_qte(), qte package’s estimator cross-sectional settings (panel data required). -based estimators staggered treatment adoption, see vignette("-estimators") vignette("staggered-adoption").
code: library(qte) library(ggplot2) set.seed(42) data(lalonde)

title: Quantile Treatment Effects in R
text: random assignment, covariate adjustment needed: unconditional quantiles treated control outcome distributions identify QTE directly. use experimental Lalonde data (lalonde.exp), treatment randomly assigned.  confidence band indicates QTE positive across much distribution, effect somewhat larger lower quantiles.

title: Quantile Treatment Effects in R
text: treatment randomly assigned, unconfoundedness assumption (conditional independence potential outcomes given covariates) provides identification. use observational Lalonde data (lalonde.psid), combines treated group experiment comparison group PSID. unc_qte() supports three estimation methods: "ipw" — inverse probability weighting (propensity score re-weighting) "" — outcome regression (conditional quantile regression) "aipw" — augmented IPW (doubly robust; recommended)

title: Staggered Treatment Adoption with the qte Package
text: many empirical settings, units adopt treatment different points time — structure called staggered treatment adoption. example, states may adopt policy different years, firms may receive intervention different times study period. -based estimators qte support staggered adoption via interface: specify outcome (yname), first-treatment-period cohort variable (gname, 0 never-treated units), calendar time (tname), unit identifier (idname). package follows approach Callaway Sant’Anna (2021), computing group-time effects ATT(g,t)ATT(g, t) every cohort gg period tt, aggregating overall, group-specific, event-study (dynamic) summaries. vignette demonstrates full applied workflow using mpdta dataset. conceptual background identification assumptions behind estimator, see vignette("-estimators"). mpdta balanced panel 500 US counties observed annually 2003 2007. outcome lemp (log county-level employment). Counties grouped first.treat, year first adopted minimum wage increase (2004, 2006, 2007); counties first.treat == 0 never adopt.
code: library(qte) library(ggplot2) set.seed(42) data(mpdta, package = "did") table(mpdta$first.treat) #>  #>    0 2004 2006 2007  #> 1545  100  200  655

title: Staggered Treatment Adoption with the qte Package
text: Setting gt_type = "qtt" returns full Quantile Treatment Effect Treated curve aggregation level. CDFs mixed across group-time cells first (using overall-ATT weights), inverted requested probs grid — avoids bias arise averaging scalar quantiles across cells.  autoplot() type = "dynamic" plots QTT selected quantile(s) across event times. plot_probs argument selects quantiles show.  Multiple quantiles can overlaid passing vector plot_probs (values must estimated probs grid):

title: Staggered Treatment Adoption with the qte Package
text: six estimators share interface. run three gt_type = "att" quick comparison overall ATT estimates: Estimates generally differ estimator relies different identifying assumption. Similarity across methods strengthens confidence findings; divergence warrants investigation assumption plausible application.
code: res_qdid <- qdid(   yname = "lemp", gname = "first.treat", tname = "year",   idname = "countyreal", data = mpdta,   gt_type = "att", biters = 50 )  res_mdid <- mdid(   yname = "lemp", gname = "first.treat", tname = "year",   idname = "countyreal", data = mpdta,   gt_type = "att", biters = 50 )  res_lou <- lou_qte(   yname = "lemp", gname = "first.treat", tname = "year",   idname = "countyreal", data = mpdta,   gt_type = "att", biters = 50 ) overall_att <- function(res, label) {   data.frame(     estimator = label,     att       = round(res$overall_results$att, 4),     se        = round(res$overall_results$se,  4)   ) }  do.call(rbind, list(   overall_att(res_att,  "CiC"),   overall_att(res_qdid, "QDiD"),   overall_att(res_mdid, "MDiD"),   overall_att(res_lou,  "Lagged-outcome unconfoundedness") )) #>                         estimator     att     se #> 1                             CiC -0.0197 0.0185 #> 2                            QDiD -0.0271 0.0159 #> 3                            MDiD -0.0305 0.0120 #> 4 Lagged-outcome unconfoundedness -0.0384 0.0193

what: Panel QTT and the pre_copula option
title: Staggered Treatment Adoption with the qte Package
text: panel_qtt() requires least two pre-treatment periods per cohort estimate copula transferred forward. pre_copula argument controls base period chosen: "long" (default): base period expands event horizon, making copula window match length post-treatment period. Requires pre-treatment periods internally consistent longer horizons. "short": always uses two periods immediately treatment, regardless event horizon.
code: res_pqtt <- panel_qtt(   yname      = "lemp",   gname      = "first.treat",   tname      = "year",   idname     = "countyreal",   data       = mpdta,   pre_copula = "long",   gt_type    = "qtt",   probs      = seq(0.1, 0.9, 0.1),   biters     = 200 ) autoplot(res_pqtt)

title: Staggered Treatment Adoption with the qte Package

title: Change in Changes — cic
text: Computes Quantile Treatment effects Treated (QTT) Average Treatment Effect Treated (ATT) using Change Changes identification strategy Athey Imbens (2006). Handles two-period data staggered treatment adoption uniformly: two-period, two-group dataset degenerate single-(g,t) case. Supports panel repeated cross sections data.

title: Distributional Difference-in-Differences — ddid

Handles two-period data staggered treatment adoption uniformly via ptetools. Requires panel data. Identification. distributional parallel trends copula restriction (rank correlation untreated potential outcomes treated control groups pre-period preserved), counterfactual distribution \(F_{Y(0),\text{post}|D=1}\) recovered adding control unit's actual change \(\Delta Y_{\text{ctrl},j}\) treated pre-period quantile control unit's rank. point-identifies QTT two time periods, contrast methods (e.g. panel_qtt) require three periods. Panel data required. Unlike cic, qdid, mdid, estimator applied repeated cross sections requires observing within-unit outcome change control unit.

Unlike CiC, QDiD, MDiD, counterfactual indexed control units, treated units. Consequently F0 ATT counterfactual term weighted w_pre_ctrl, individual treatment effect distribution (Fte) returned. Panel data required. estimator needs actual change \(\Delta Y_{\text{ctrl},j}\) control unit, requires observing units periods.

title: Lagged Outcome Unconfoundedness QTT — lou_qte
text: Estimates Quantile Treatment Effect Treated (QTT) Average Treatment Effect Treated (ATT) lagged-outcome unconfoundedness assumption staggered treatment adoption. key identifying assumption \(Y_{g,t}(0) \perp D \mid X, Y_{\text{pre}}\), .e., conditional observed covariates pre-treatment outcome, treatment good randomly assigned within cohort-period cell. Estimation operates (g,t) level: cohort \(g\) post-treatment period \(t\), cross-sectional comparison made treated group -yet-treated (never-treated) comparison group, adjusting covariates (optionally) pre-treatment outcome. Group-time estimates aggregated overall, dynamic, group-specific summaries. Three estimation methods available: "ipw" Propensity-score reweighting. Control units reweighted \(\hat p(X,Y_{\text{pre}})/(1-\hat p(X,Y_{\text{pre}}))\) approximate covariate distribution treated group. "" Outcome regression. quantile regression model fit control units' post-period outcomes function \((X, Y_{\text{pre}})\), predicted treated units construct counterfactual distribution (Melly 2006; Chernozhukov, Fernandez-Val, Melly 2013). "aipw" Doubly-robust augmented IPW. Combines propensity score outcome models. Consistent either model correctly specified. lagged_outcome_cov = FALSE xformula = ~1, three methods reduce simple distribution comparison within (g,t) cell, consistent unconditional unconfoundedness. ATT analogue (without QTT) staggered adoption lagged-outcome unconfoundedness developed Callaway (2023).

title: Mean Difference-in-Differences — mdid
text: Computes Quantile Treatment effects Treated (QTT) Average Treatment Effect Treated (ATT) using Mean Difference--Differences identification strategy. Handles two-period data staggered treatment adoption uniformly. Supports panel repeated cross sections data. Identification. MDiD assumes counterfactual distribution untreated potential outcomes location shift treated group's pre-treatment distribution. size shift mean \(\Delta = E[Y_{\text{post}}|D=0] - E[Y_{\text{pre}}|D=0]\), \(Q_{Y(0),\text{post}|D=1}(\tau) = Q_{Y,\text{pre}|D=1}(\tau) + \Delta\). stronger parallel trends means alone: additionally requires shape treated group's outcome distribution unchanged counterfactual. MDiD special case QDiD applies single mean shift rather rank-specific distributional shift. Covariate adjustment. xformula specified, scalar shift replaced unit-specific conditional mean shift \(\Delta(X_i) = E[Y_{\text{post}}|D=0,X_i] - E[Y_{\text{pre}}|D=0,X_i]\), estimated weighted OLS. counterfactual treated unit \(\) \(Y_{\text{pre},} + \Delta(X_i)\), unconditional counterfactual distribution empirical CDF values. law iterated expectations, consistently estimates \(F_{Y(0),\text{post}|D=1}\).

title: Mean Difference-in-Differences: group-time estimator — mdid_gt
text: Computes MDiD ATT counterfactual outcome distribution single 2x2 (pre/post x treated/control) data subset. Serves directly attgt_fun argument ptetools::pte. Panel vs. repeated cross sections detected automatically whether unit ids appear periods. Identification. MDiD assumes counterfactual distribution untreated potential outcomes treated group post period location shift treated group's pre-period distribution: $$F_{Y(0),\text{post}|D=1}(y) = F_{Y,\text{pre}|D=1}(y - \Delta)$$ \(\Delta = E[Y_{\text{post}}|D=0] - E[Y_{\text{pre}}|D=0]\) mean (change mean outcomes untreated group). counterfactual quantile function therefore \(Q_{Y(0),\text{post}|D=1}(\tau) = Q_{Y,\text{pre}|D=1}(\tau) + \Delta\). Covariate adjustment. covariates, scalar shift \(\Delta\) replaced unit-specific conditional mean shift \(\Delta(X_i) = E[Y_{\text{post}}|D=0, X_i] - E[Y_{\text{pre}}|D=0, X_i]\), estimated weighted OLS control group period. counterfactual treated pre-period unit \(\) \(Y_{\text{pre},} + \Delta(X_i)\). unconditional counterfactual distribution empirical CDF shifted values, law iterated expectations consistently estimates \(F_{Y(0),\text{post}|D=1}(y) = \int F_{Y,\text{pre}|D=1,X=x}(y - \Delta(x))\,dF_{X|D=1}(x)\).

title: Panel QTT (Callaway-Li 2019) — panel_qtt
text: Computes Quantile Treatment Effects Treated (QTT) Average Treatment Effect Treated (ATT) using three-period panel identification strategy Callaway Li (2019). Handles two-period (one post + two pre-treatment periods) staggered treatment adoption via ptetools. Requires panel data groups. Identification. copula stability assumption distributional parallel trends changes, counterfactual distribution \(F_{Y(0)^{\text{post}}|D=1}\) recovered three periods panel data without requiring rank invariance. pre_copula. Controls pre-treatment periods used copula-transfer base post-treatment cells (g, g+e): "long" (default) pre2 = 2*(g - anticipation) - (g+e) - 2, matching copula window event horizon e. Cells pre2 exist data excluded aggregation two-step weight renormalization. "short" pre2 = g - anticipation - 2, always anchored treatment onset. Pre-test placebo cells always use sliding base regardless pre_copula.

title: Panel QTT: group-time estimator (Callaway-Li 2019) — panel_qtt_gt
text: Computes panel QTT counterfactual outcome distribution single (g,t) cell using three-period copula-stability estimator Callaway Li (2019). Serves directly attgt_fun argument ptetools::pte. Identification. copula stability $$C(F_{Y^{\text{pre2}}|D=1},\, F_{\Delta Y^{\text{pre}}|D=1}) =         C(F_{Y^{\text{pre1}}(0)|D=1},\, F_{\Delta Y^{\text{post}}(0)|D=1})$$ distributional parallel trends changes $$F_{\Delta Y^{\text{post}}(0)|D=1} = F_{\Delta Y^{\text{ctrl}}}$$ counterfactual outcome treated unit \(\) \(kcf_i = L_i + C_i,\) \(L_i = Q_{Y^{\text{pre1}}|D=1}(u_i)\) treated pre1 quantile rank \(u_i = F_{Y^{\text{pre2}}|D=1}(Y^{\text{pre2}}_i)\) (Rosenblatt transform), \(C_i = Q_{\Delta Y^{\text{ctrl}}}(v_i)\) control change rank \(v_i = F_{\Delta Y^{\text{pre}}|D=1}(\Delta Y^{\text{pre}}_i)\). goal distributional: \(\{kcf_i\}\) sample \(F_{Y(0)^{\text{post}}|D=1}\), individual counterfactuals. Panel data required groups. Three periods treated (pre2, pre1, post) two control (pre1, post).

title: QTT Aggregation for pre_copula =
text: Custom aggregation_fun ptetools::pte handles two-step weight renormalization needed pre_copula = "long": (g,t) cells structurally uncomputable (pre2 exist data), cells must excluded aggregation proper weight redistribution. Two-step weights (overall): Identify surviving groups (least one valid post-treatment cell); renormalize \(p_g\) among groups . Within surviving group, assign equal weight \(p_g / |\text{valid cells } g|\) valid post-treatment cell. Dynamic (event-study) weights: event time e, renormalize \(p_g\) among groups (g, g+e) valid. Error behaviour: structurally valid cell returns NA att, function errors immediately — NAs indicate bug panel_qtt_gt, structural limitation.

what: qte: A package for computing quantile treatment effects — qte-package
title: qte: A package for computing quantile treatment effects — qte-package
text: Provides several methods computing Quantile Treatment Effect (QTE) Quantile Treatment Effect Treated (QTT). main cases covered () treatment randomly assigned, (ii) treatment good randomly assigned conditioning covariates (selection observables) using methods Firpo (2007) doi:10.1111/j.1468-0262.2007.00738.x , (iii) identification based Difference Differences assumption, support several varieties including Athey Imbens (2006) doi:10.1111/j.1468-0262.2006.00668.x , Callaway Li (2019) doi:10.3982/QE935 , Callaway, Li, Oka (2018) doi:10.1016/j.jeconom.2018.06.008 . Version 2.0 adds unified staggered treatment adoption API (built 'ptetools') -based estimators, well new lagged-outcome unconfoundedness estimator ('lou_qte').

what: Three-Period Subset for Panel QTT — three_period_subset
title: Three-Period Subset for Panel QTT — three_period_subset
text: Custom subset_fun ptetools::pte pulls three time periods (g,t) cell: pre2, pre1 = g - anticipation - 1, post = tp. pre2 chosen depends pre_copula: "long" (default) pre2 = 2*(g - anticipation) - tp - 2, copula-transfer window matches event horizon e = tp - (g - anticipation). Cells pre2 exist data return empty data panel_qtt_gt returns NA. "short" pre2 = g - anticipation - 2 (fixed treatment onset regardless e). pre-treatment placebo cells (tp < g - anticipation), base always slides backwards regardless pre_copula: pre2 = tp - 2, pre1 = tp - 1.  panel_qtt() sets required_pre_periods = 2 minimum tp tlist third period, guaranteeing tp - 2 always valid period.

title: unc_qte — unc_qte
text: Estimates Quantile Treatment Effect (QTE) Quantile Treatment Effect Treated (QTT) unconfoundedness, also known selection observables. key identifying assumption \((Y(0), Y(1)) \perp D \mid X\), .e., potential outcomes independent treatment conditional covariates \(X\). Three estimation methods available via est_method: "ipw" Propensity-score reweighting (Firpo 2007). propensity score \(p(X) = P(D=1|X)\) estimated logit probit used reweight sample covariate distributions treated untreated groups match. QTE, groups reweighted toward population distribution; QTT, untreated group reweighted toward treated covariate distribution. "" Outcome regression via quantile regression inversion. rq fit dense internal grid \(\tau\) values, yielding conditional quantile function \(Q_{Y|X,D=j}(\tau)\) arm \(j\). marginal distribution recovered averaging conditional quantile functions empirical covariate distribution (Melly 2006; Chernozhukov, Fernandez-Val, Melly 2013). "aipw" Doubly-robust augmented IPW. Combines propensity score model conditional quantile outcome model. CDF estimator consistent either propensity score outcome model correctly specified (semiparametric efficiency correct). xformla = ~1 (default), three methods reduce simple quantile differences treated untreated groups, consistent unconditional unconfoundedness. Covariates required covariate-adjusted estimation. Standard errors uniform confidence bands computed via empirical bootstrap. Setting biters = 0 planned future version skip inference return point estimates .

title: unc_qte — unc_qte
text: yname character; name outcome variable data. dname character; name binary treatment indicator data (1 = treated, 0 = untreated). data data.frame containing analysis data. xformla one-sided formula covariates used propensity score /outcome model, e.g. ~ age + (age^2) + education. Default ~1 uses covariates (simple quantile differences). weightsname character; name column data containing sampling weights. Default NULL applies equal weights. probs numeric vector quantile levels evaluate QTE QTT. Default seq(0.05, 0.95, 0.05). alp significance level confidence intervals. Default 0.05. biters number bootstrap iterations standard errors confidence bands. Default 100. cband logical; TRUE (default), report uniform confidence band (simultaneous quantiles probs) addition pointwise intervals. boot_type bootstrap variant. Currently "empirical" supported. method propensity score link function: "logit" (default) "probit". Used est_method = "ipw" "aipw". est_method estimation method; one : "ipw" (default) — inverse propensity weighting "" — outcome regression via quantile regression inversion "aipw" — doubly-robust augmented IPW target target parameter; one : "qte" (default) — population QTE: \(Q_{Y(1)}(\tau) - Q_{Y(0)}(\tau)\) "qtt" — QTT (effect treated): \(Q_{Y(1)|D=1}(\tau) - Q_{Y(0)|D=1}(\tau)\) cl number cores parallel bootstrap. Default 1 (sequential).

title: qte 2.0.0
text: largest update package’s history. API changed substantially — please read section upgrading.

title: qte 2.0.0
text: unc_qte() — cross-sectional QTE QTT unconfoundedness. Supports IPW, outcome regression, doubly robust (AIPW) estimation. Uses yname/dname interface consistent /ptetools convention. Replaces ci.qte() ci.qtet(), retained deprecated wrappers. cic() / cic_gt() — Change Changes estimator staggered treatment adoption (Athey Imbens 2006). Supports panel repeated cross sections data, returns either ATT (gt_type = "att") full QTT curve (gt_type = "qtt"). Replaces CiC(), retained deprecated wrapper. qdid() / qdid_gt() — Quantile Difference Differences estimator staggered treatment adoption (Callaway, Li, Oka 2018). Replaces QDiD(), retained deprecated wrapper. mdid() / mdid_gt() — Mean Difference Differences estimator staggered treatment adoption (Callaway Li 2019). Replaces MDiD(), retained deprecated wrapper. ddid() / ddid_gt() — Distributional Difference Differences estimator staggered treatment adoption (Callaway Li 2019). Replaces ddid2(), retained deprecated wrapper. panel_qtt() / panel_qtt_gt() — Panel QTT estimator using copula transfer across pre-treatment periods (Callaway Li 2019), staggered treatment adoption support. pre_copula argument controls whether rolling ("long", default) fixed ("short") base period used transfer copula. Replaces panel.qtet(), retained deprecated wrapper. lou_qte() — Lagged-outcome unconfoundedness QTT estimator staggered treatment adoption. Uses lagged outcome control variable identify QTT. New; prior equivalent package.
